About This Story

In this powerful story, three girls make a change. Maya is 15 and fights for climate action. Emma, 11, collects 12,000 books for diversity. Sofia, 8, writes to the President about water. They go from lonely outsiders to leaders. Their stories show that one person can make a difference. This is an inspiring story about proving that young people can change the world.

Imagine being fifteen years old and thinking you are too small to make a difference. Imagine sitting alone outside a government building with a hand-painted sign. Imagine your parents trying to stop you. Your classmates refusing to join you. Strangers looking at you with pity. But you keep going anyway. This is the story of three girls who proved that one small person can change the world.

Many people believe that young people cannot make a difference. They think that children are too small or too young to create change. This story proves that belief wrong.
